name: USS SC-1275 (SC-1275)
events: 2 Aug 1945 Around 1500 K/2, USS Devilfish (T/Lt.Cdr. S.S. Mann, Jr., USN) and USS Torsk (T/Cdr. B.E. Lewellen, USN) departed from Guam for their 4th and 2nd war patrols. USS Devilfish was ordered to patrol off the Bonin Islands and USS Torsk was to patrol in the Sea of Japan.
They were escorted until around 1845 K/2 by USS SC-1275 (Lt.(jg) M.L. White, USNR).
The submarines parted company with each other around 1530 K/5.
